meta.name: Arroz con Pollo (Peruvian-Style)
meta.subtitle: Cilantro-aji green rice with braised chicken, peas, carrots, and beer stock

source.name: SSA Canonical Catalogs
notes: Peruvian arroz con pollo typically uses a green cilantro/aji base and is braised together so the rice absorbs the flavor.
This recipe is written to be deterministic and adaptable to different cookware (Dutch oven, heavy pot, rice cooker finish).

summary: Peruvian arroz con pollo is a one-pot chicken-and-rice dish with a vivid green cilantro base, lightly spicy ají, and braised rice that drinks up savory stock (often with beer).

yield.notes: Serves 6 as a main; 8 with sides.

signals.freezerFriendly: Rice texture softens after freezing but is workable for meal prep.

heat.note: Ají amarillo is optional; keep mild for family style.

optional.label: Rice cooker
optional.notes: You can finish the rice in a rice cooker after browning chicken and building the base.

notes: Beer is optional; substitute extra stock. Alcohol cooks off but trace may remain depending on simmer time.

ingredients.label: Chicken thighs, bone-in skin-on (or drumsticks)
ingredients.notes: Bone-in gives the best flavor. You can use boneless thighs; reduce braise time slightly.

ingredients.label: Kosher salt
ingredients.notes: Split between chicken seasoning and final rice seasoning.

ingredients.label: Ground cumin
ingredients.notes: Optional but common in many home versions.

ingredients.label: Cilantro leaves and tender stems
ingredients.notes: Packed cups. This is what creates the signature green color.

ingredients.label: Spinach (optional, for deeper green)
ingredients.notes: Optional; helps color if cilantro is mild or small.

ingredients.label: Ají amarillo paste
ingredients.notes: Optional; adds classic Peruvian fruit-pepper flavor. Start small for mild.

ingredients.label: Lager beer (optional)
ingredients.notes: Traditional in many recipes; substitute stock if preferred.

ingredients.label: Long-grain white rice, rinsed until water runs mostly clear
ingredients.notes: Rinsing helps keep the rice fluffy, not gummy.

ingredients.label: Red bell pepper, sliced (optional)
ingredients.notes: Some versions include pimiento/roasted pepper strips or bell pepper.

steps.title: Blend the green cilantro base
instructions: Add cilantro (and optional spinach) to a blender with 1/2 cup chicken stock (or water).
Blend until very smooth and bright green; scrape down as needed.
Set aside; this is the signature flavor/color base.
checks: Base is smooth (no large leaf pieces).
Color is vivid green (spinach helps if cilantro is pale).

steps.title: Season the chicken
instructions: Pat chicken dry for better browning.
Season all sides with 1 1/2 tsp salt, black pepper, and optional cumin.
Let sit while you heat the pot.
checks: Chicken surface is dry and evenly seasoned.

steps.title: Brown the chicken
instructions: Heat oil in a heavy pot or Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Brown chicken skin-side down until golden (work in batches if needed).
Flip and brown the second side briefly.
Remove chicken to a plate; keep the rendered fat in the pot.
checks: Chicken has good golden color (fond on bottom is OK).
Fat is rendered (especially with skin-on thighs).

steps.title: Sauté onion and garlic
instructions: Lower heat to medium.
Add onion and cook until softened, scraping up browned bits.
Add garlic and cook 30–60 seconds until fragrant (do not burn).
checks: Onions are softened and sweet-smelling; garlic is fragrant, not browned.

steps.title: Toast rice and build the braising liquid
instructions: Stir in rinsed rice and toast 1–2 minutes to coat in aromatics and fat.
Stir in optional ají amarillo paste and cook 30 seconds to bloom.
Add beer (if using) and simmer 1 minute to reduce slightly.
Add remaining chicken stock and the blended green base; stir well.
checks: Rice looks lightly translucent at edges after toasting.
Liquid is evenly green with no clumps.

steps.title: Nestle chicken and braise until rice is tender
instructions: Return chicken (and any juices) to the pot, nestling pieces into the rice mixture.
Add diced carrots now (peas go later).
Bring to a gentle simmer, then cover and reduce heat to low.
Cook covered until rice is tender and chicken is cooked through.
Add peas in the last 5 minutes so they stay bright.
checks: Rice grains are tender (no hard center).
Chicken reaches safe doneness (165°F / 74°C in thickest part).

steps.title: Rest, fluff, and serve
instructions: Turn off heat and let the pot rest covered 10 minutes to finish steaming.
Fluff rice gently with a fork, keeping chicken pieces intact.
Taste and adjust salt (use remaining ~3/4 tsp as needed depending on stock).
Garnish with cilantro and serve with lime wedges.
checks: Rice is fluffy, not wet; if slightly wet, rest uncovered 3–5 minutes.
Seasoning is balanced; finish with lime at the table.

refrigerate.notes: Cool quickly, store covered. Reheat gently with a splash of stock/water to loosen rice.

freeze.notes: Freeze in portions. Rice softens when thawed; still useful for meal prep.

notes: Cool rice promptly (spread on a tray for faster cooling if needed).
Reheat leftovers to steaming hot (165°F / 74°C recommended).

makeAheadNotes: Blend cilantro base up to 24 hours ahead; refrigerate tightly covered.
Dice carrots/onion ahead for faster weeknight assembly.
scalingNotes: Double batch works well in a large Dutch oven; keep simmer gentle to prevent scorching.
For boneless thighs, begin checking doneness 5 minutes earlier.
weeknightTips: Use boneless thighs + pre-chopped mirepoix to reduce active time.
Skip beer and use all stock for faster, simpler build.

notes: Liquid ratio: This recipe targets fluffy long-grain rice. If your pot runs hot or you see scorching, add 1/4 cup stock and keep heat low.
If cilantro flavor is strong/bitter, reduce cilantro to 1 1/2 cups and include spinach for color.
For a more classic Peruvian aroma, add a bay leaf when simmering (remove before serving).
Use 2 lb boneless skinless thighs. Brown quickly, then braise 18–22 minutes total.
Begin checking rice at 18 minutes; add peas at minute ~15–17.
Skip beer. Increase chicken stock by 1/2 cup (total 3 cups).
Add 1 tsp vinegar or extra lime at the table for brightness if desired.
Increase ají amarillo paste to 2 tbsp total (split between rice base and a small spoon into the green blend).
Serve with ají verde for extra heat control.
Add sliced bell pepper with onions.
Add 1/2 cup corn kernels with peas in the last 5 minutes.
Rice dishes should be cooled promptly and stored cold to reduce risk of Bacillus cereus growth.
Reheat leftovers thoroughly and avoid repeated warm-cool cycles.
